text: Every time you get a 5 star artifact these are the possible values each sub stat will have and that they can obtain at every 4 levels. When upgrading artifacts there's RNG choosing which of the 4 sub stats to upgrade, and then another RNG to pick which of those 4 possible values will be added to that sub stat.

text: Build out full team first (different elements, at least one decent healer) so you have heavy hitter team if you ever need one, even if you vary teams regularly. text: I would suggest going nation by nation. start with the archon quest and explore as you do so, atleast getting waypoints. when you’ve finished that I’d suggest doing all the other smaller quests, namely character story quests and world quests as some parts of exploration are locked behind these. finally I’d recommend you explore that nation before repeating the process for the next region. this keeps everything in chronological order and ensures you don’t just neglect a nation !! of course it’s up to you but this keeps things organized. also fyi daily server reset which I’m pretty sure is at 4AM GMT-5 time however I’m clueless except for knowing it’s 7:30PM ACDT time !! Make sure to complete these daily as they give 60 primos and 1500 adventurer exp !!

text: Hey all! I started this game a week ago and still feel a bit lost when it comes to items that I should use/save for later, what chars to level, etc. So my question; what are tips/tricks/things you wish you had known when you just started this game.

text: leveling up artifacts. i was so stingy with my resources i hold on to everything, even leveling those 2* and 3* aritfacts. it really make my time easier, especially the flower and the feather, until you get better ones. flat stats is really useful in early days
label: analysis

text: Do daily commissions. Use your daily resin. Only wish on characters you really like (ignore power/tier lists) I'd suggest looking through all of them in game under the character archive, seeing their animations, then watching their trailers on youtube for the characters you like best so know who you for sure want to pull for when they show up on the limited banner. Don't worry about artifacts til way later on and you start to struggle beating bosses. ...I would focus on leveling up only a few characters you like. It's better to have 1 or 2 level 90 characters with full talents leveled up than to have 10 characters all at level 40. Also never wish on the weapon banner, it's a waste. Getting Characters are way more fun than just seeing larger numbers from having ""better weapons"". Also, pick up every sparkly thing you come across in the world, some day you'll be glad you did when a character you get needs like 100 of a certain flower.
label: analysis

text: Don’t worry about artifacts at the beginning, just focus on leveling the character, talents and their weapon. You can equip them for their set effects I suppose and level if you struggle, but they only really become important later on and it’s not recommended to farm for them before AR45. Also focus on one team, else it will be really hard to keep up with materials. (In my beginning I just put characters in a team I liked and got through the overworld fairly fine) I would recommend doing the Archon Quests (Main Story) you unlock some new characters from it later on and whole areas like Inazuma. Don’t feel pressured to blast through it tho! Just take things at your own pace for now and enjoy the different game elements. If you have any specific question, feel free to DM me I’d be happy to help :)
label: analysis

text: Early game, it's quite cheap and easy to level characters up to about level 40 or 50, so just experiment and level up every character you get. It does get more expensive to level up when you get nearer the endgame, but that's the point where you'll want to start looking to focus in on building a handful of characters for a specific team or two. You should know what you're doing a bit more by then.
label: analysis

text: Nod Krai main DPS (Neffer/Flins/Zibai) ideally want all 50~60% CR with the exception of Zibai IF you have C6 illuga in team. Your Flins lands at 67% CR after Night of Sky set applies, my recommendation is increasing your CR without having to sacrifice your CD/ATK, I assume you are at the same spot as OP without his Signature/CR-Weapon. Use the DPS Test Dummy in Wonderland and decrease your CD while increasing your CR to that of a CR circlet (via Stat Editor) and see how your Flins Performs. This way you can test performance without having to farm for a Artefact and if you like it, you can still farm for it.

text: Since everyone is different and each has a route they find the "best" the one i go with is, teleport to brightcrown mountains- rake down the camp nearby-drop down take out the slimes-carry on and repeat with the 2 groups and end by beating the ruin guard- next, teleport to wolvendom, 2 groups and camp there, 1 small one leading to the slimes, then a jaunt to another group with rocks - drop down to the Cecilla garden, take out two groups, and that's for monstadt for Liyue teleport north of Minyun village small camp- group -4 ice slimes - jaunt south one big camp one more group, water slimes, and then teleport to Guyun domain, head up the mountain BEAT THE S**T out of the abyss mage trio, glide to the small island, swim back to shore, take out the group with the fire mage and finish with the Lingu pass triple mage + fatui agent

text: Story will take a long time to catch up to. New region will have new characters that will benefit your account the most. But you can’t unlock their materials until you advance in the story.
label: analysis

text: I would avoid the Fontaine Chronicled Wish banner entirely until you understand things a little more, especially if this is your first gacha game. As for the others, you could go for the characters if you like them, be sure to try the trial for them under the events page (maybe more than the minimum time too), but it's better if you do some research to find characters you like first (bonus points if they're running on banner soon or very strong) Depends how much you care about 'meta' and being as strong as possible, or just clearing endgame content consistently (not necessarily the same thing) And DON'T spend primogems on the standard or beginner banner. Only use the blue orbs, but generally feel free to spend those whenever you get them.
label: analysis

text: Do not rush to make wishes on the limited / chronicled banners. Since you just started the game, you don’t know what characters you like, or elements, or weapons, or play styles. It would suck if you pulled on a current banner and wasted resources on a character you don’t enjoy playing. Play the game with the free characters you get (plus anyone you can get on the Beginners’ banner or the permanent banner with blue fates (NEVER primogems)), and choose a free standard character (assuming that’s available to you (there are a lot of posts here with advice for that selection)) and see what you like! Maybe you hate bow characters because you hate aiming, or maybe you hate claymore characters because they’re slower. You probably don’t know yet, so give it some time! In the meantime, just play the game at your own pace and save the primogems for the future, once you know what you want and how you like to play :)
label: analysis

text: I'm assuming you're a new player so I really think guides will help you build a foundation in understanding characters and the game's damage mechanics. And most people just use guides/ask others or use similar teams But the more you end up understanding the game, eventually you'll be able to just kinda know what various characters provide and how it influences or doesn't influence your damage and gameplay.
label: analysis

text: You just have to build around reactions now. Think about how each reaction works and then consider the characters and their roles in it. Like are they on or off field, how do they apply their element and how often, whether they will be the aura or the trigger. Good things to focus on are trying to get an on-field DPS of each element and then supports who can fit on reaction teams for them. Look around the wiki for more info about the mechanics I mentioned and character kit details.
label: analysis

text: i recommend keqingmain website and then watch a lot of genshin guides for team building to get a grasp of some things. If you don't know what's smth is make sure to looking it up so you can comprehend what's being said. You're also gonna have to understand how reactions work. For now you should watch guides on the characters you wanna make teams for but make sure to read their kits so you understand how things work and so you can remember what those characters do for future teams. Overtake guides on youtube and keqingmain are you best solution
label: analysis

text: Build a team with a on-fielder that uses their on-field time well (yanfei, sayu, noelle, you get the gist), and 3 others that don't need a lot of field time, whether they buff/heal/shield your team or do damage from off field (xiangling, kaeya, bennett, zhongli, etc)

text: Well, there's a saying in gacha community "this a marathon, not a sprint". I don't think you're supposed to perfectly build 4 characters in a patch, at least in this game. I see two ways to look at it. First, it's done so you won't burn out or straight up nolife it. Second way is: so you login every day and form a habit and won't leave until servers shut down. Which is bread and butter of retention in gacha games. Think about it, what will you do if you achieved all you wanted in a game? The answer might be that you quit. Hoyo wants to prolong your stay as much as they can. That's why we still don't have a skip button while almost every other gacha game on the market already has it. The scedule on domains exists solely for this reason too.
label: hot take

text: It all started with Neuvi and Arle. Pioneer of breaking 100k dps at c0r0. Then it became the norm in natlan. Then came Mavuika, who was supposed to bring the new ceiling to around 135k. But with her insane ever evolving combo, it went past it to 170k. Hoyo didn't like it and instead of leaving Mavuika's ceiling as an exception decided to make that the new standard. text: I've been playing Genshin from the start -since day 2 of Mondstat- and for the most part I love this game, however Natlan, power creep, and lack of representation are killing this game. To start, I’ve never been one to 36 star the abyss every time, however in the more recent abyss I can barely even clear it let alone achieve 36 stars. I’ve poured over $1,000 into Genshin in order to play with my favorite characters, and despite lvl 90 characters, good relics, and 5 star weapons I am still unable to clear. And this would be ok if the other parts of the game were not falling apart. Natlan was such a disappointment, and especially because we just came from Fontaine which was hard to live up too. This is evident by a lack in creativity in character kit designs and the night soul mechanic. More and more I’m seeing people drop the game and it worried me, but this patch finally made me understand. The cast of Natlan was for the most part boring, and despite Natlan being based of Latin, indigenous, and African cultures, Hoyoverse absolutely failed to have any brown characters other than iansan. I’m completely white and I was even disappointed, Natlan’s culture felt like a culture project written by a 5th grader the night before it was due. This is even more apparent when you look at inazuma and how Japanese culture was depicted, Natlan felt like a joke. Additionally the only character that I liked to the extent of wanting to pull for them was Citlali, and Hoyo also decided it was a good idea to put 2 new limited 5 stars on the same banner.-this is beyond money hungry, it’s predatory, it makes me sad to see how far this game that I love has fallen, and at this point the only reason I’m playing is because of the main storyline and I have to see how it ends. So in the end Ive realized that I’m actually only playing because of the suspense, storyline, and world building of the past version. I feel like All of the things that made Genshin great are just gone. finally, I’m not sure if I’m going crazy or if anyone else feels like the compounding issues of the game have left a husk. So if you have any thoughts, or disagree with me please let me know since I want to know if this is just a me thing or not.
label: hot take
